input.title: Learning Management System
input.description: Learning Management System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Django, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Student Registration and Enrollment
requirements: Students can register with email and password
Students can browse available courses
Students can enroll in courses
System tracks enrollment history

FR-02.title: Course Creation by Instructors
requirements: Instructors can create courses with title, description, and syllabus
Courses support multiple modules and lessons
Instructors can upload video lectures and documents
Course can be published or kept as draft

FR-03.title: Video Lecture Playback
requirements: Students can watch video lectures within the platform
Video player supports pause, seek, and playback speed
System tracks video progress per student
Resume from last watched position

FR-04.title: Quiz and Assessment System
requirements: Instructors can create quizzes with multiple choice questions
Quizzes can have time limits
System auto-grades multiple choice quizzes
Students can view quiz results and correct answers

FR-05.title: Assignment Submission
requirements: Instructors can create assignments with due dates
Students can submit files and text for assignments
Late submissions are flagged
Instructors can grade and provide feedback

FR-06.title: Progress Tracking Dashboard
requirements: Students can view course completion percentage
Dashboard shows completed and pending lessons
Progress bar for each enrolled course
Certificate generated on course completion

FR-07.title: Discussion Forum
requirements: Each course has a discussion forum
Students and instructors can create threads
Users can reply to threads
Instructor answers are highlighted

FR-08.title: Course Rating and Review
requirements: Students can rate courses with 1-5 stars
Students can write text reviews
Average rating displayed on course page
Only enrolled students can review

FR-09.title: Payment and Subscription
requirements: Courses can be free or paid
Payment via credit card or PayPal
Subscription plans for unlimited access
Refund within 30 days of purchase

FR-10.title: Admin Analytics Dashboard
requirements: Admin can view total students, courses, and revenue
Charts for enrollment trends over time
Top-rated and most enrolled courses
Instructor performance metrics

FR-01.title: Plan Student Registration and Enrollment Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for student registration and enrollment and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Students can register with email and password; Students can browse available courses; Students can enroll in courses.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for student registration and enrollment is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for student registration and enrollment are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for student registration and enrollment can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Student Registration and Enrollment Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for student registration and enrollment on React, Django, PostgreSQL. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Students can register with email and password; Students can browse available courses; Students can enroll in courses.
acceptance_criteria: Core student registration and enrollment business rules are implemented for Learning Management System
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for student registration and enrollment
API/service behavior for student registration and enrollment is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Student Registration and Enrollment Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for student registration and enrollment with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The student registration and enrollment user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for student registration and enrollment behave correctly
The student registration and enrollment UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Integrate External Dependencies for Student Registration and Enrollment
FR-01.description: Implement the external integration points used by student registration and enrollment, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. Add retry-safe behavior, timeout handling, observability, and fallback behavior for downstream failures.
acceptance_criteria: External dependencies used by student registration and enrollment are connected through stable integration layers
Timeout, retry, and failure handling for student registration and enrollment integrations are implemented and testable
Secrets, credentials, and integration logs for student registration and enrollment are handled securely

FR-01.title: Write Student Registration and Enrollment Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for student registration and enrollment. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for student registration and enrollment are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for student registration and enrollment are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical student registration and enrollment scenarios

FR-02.description: Apply security controls to course creation by instructors. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for course creation by instructors are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in course creation by instructors are audited and protected against misuse
